questionText: Você está contando ao seu médico que tem dormido mal há semanas e que isso está afetando seu desempenho no trabalho. Qual frase expressa melhor essa situação em inglês?
correctAnswer: I've been struggling with persistent insomnia for weeks now, and it's taking a serious toll on my performance at work.
options: I've been struggling with persistent insomnia for weeks now, and it's taking a serious toll on my performance at work.
I don't sleep well and my work is bad because of it.
I have been not sleeping and working is difficult for me lately.
My sleep is not good and the work suffers from this problem I have.

questionText: Você quer pedir ao farmacêutico uma alternativa genérica para um medicamento prescrito que está muito caro. Como você expressaria isso de forma adequada?
correctAnswer: Would you happen to have a generic equivalent for this prescription? The brand-name version is quite pricey.
options: Give me a cheaper medicine, this one costs too much money for me.
Would you happen to have a generic equivalent for this prescription? The brand-name version is quite pricey.
I want another medicine that is not so expensive like this one here.
Can you change my medicine to a less costly one from the generic?

questionText: Você está explicando a um amigo estrangeiro que precisa fazer exames de rotina todos os anos como medida preventiva. Qual opção expressa isso com mais naturalidade?
correctAnswer: I make a point of getting my annual check-up every year — it's just good preventive care.
options: I always do my exams every year because prevention is important for health.
I make a point of getting my annual check-up every year — it's just good preventive care.
Every year I go to the doctor for doing routine tests for prevention.
I do yearly tests at the doctor to prevent diseases from happening to me.

questionText: Você quer recomendar a um colega que está estressado que experimente meditação como forma de aliviar a ansiedade. Qual expressão seria mais natural em inglês?
correctAnswer: You might want to give meditation a shot — it can work wonders for anxiety relief.
options: You should try meditation because it helps anxiety a lot and it is good for you.
Meditation is something good for anxiety, you need to try it very soon.
You might want to give meditation a shot — it can work wonders for anxiety relief.
I recommend you to do meditation because of your anxiety and stress problems.

questionText: Você está descrevendo para o médico uma dor que piora gradualmente ao longo do dia e melhora depois de descansar. Como expressaria isso?
correctAnswer: The pain tends to build up gradually throughout the day and then eases off after I've had some rest.
options: The pain tends to build up gradually throughout the day and then eases off after I've had some rest.
My pain gets more during the day and becomes better when I rest after.
I feel pain that increases in the day and decreases when I take rest.
The pain is more in the day time and goes away a little after resting for me.

questionText: Você quer informar ao médico que tem histórico familiar de doenças cardíacas e que isso o preocupa. Qual seria a forma mais adequada de comunicar isso?
correctAnswer: There's a strong family history of heart disease on my side, which is something I've always been mindful of.
options: In my family many people had heart problems and I am worried about this for me.
Heart disease is in my family history and I think about this with worry.
There's a strong family history of heart disease on my side, which is something I've always been mindful of.
My family has heart diseases and I am concerned that I can have it too someday.

questionText: Você quer pedir ao médico para explicar de forma mais simples um diagnóstico que não entendeu bem. Como faria isso educadamente?
correctAnswer: I'm sorry, I didn't quite follow that — could you break it down in layman's terms for me?
options: I'm sorry, I didn't quite follow that — could you break it down in layman's terms for me?
I don't understand, can you explain again but more simple for me please?
Please say it again more simply because I did not understand the medical words.
Can you repeat what you said but in easier words because I am confused now?

questionText: Você está aconselhando um amigo a não ignorar sintomas persistentes e procurar ajuda médica. Qual frase transmite melhor esse conselho?
correctAnswer: You really shouldn't brush off those symptoms — it's worth getting them checked out sooner rather than later.
options: Please go to the doctor because your symptoms are not good and you should not ignore them.
You really shouldn't brush off those symptoms — it's worth getting them checked out sooner rather than later.
Don't ignore your symptoms, they are important and you need to see a doctor very soon.
Your symptoms are something you should not forget and going to the doctor is necessary.

questionText: Você está descrevendo ao nutricionista seus hábitos alimentares, informando que come de forma irregular e muitas vezes pula refeições por conta do trabalho. Como diria isso?
correctAnswer: My eating habits are pretty erratic — I often skip meals because of work commitments.
options: I eat at different times always and sometimes I don't eat because I work a lot.
My eating habits are pretty erratic — I often skip meals because of work commitments.
I have bad eating habits because work makes me not eat at the correct times.
Because of work I don't eat well and sometimes I forget to have my meals.

questionText: Você quer perguntar ao médico se os efeitos colaterais que está sentindo de um medicamento são normais ou se deve parar de tomá-lo. Qual opção seria mais apropriada?
correctAnswer: Are these side effects I'm experiencing within the normal range, or should I discontinue the medication?
options: The medicine is giving me problems. Should I stop taking it or is it normal to feel this?
Are these side effects I'm experiencing within the normal range, or should I discontinue the medication?
I feel bad with this medicine. Is this normal or I need to stop using it now?
Can you tell me if my side effects are okay or if I should stop to take the medication?

questionText: Você está contando a um amigo sobre sua recente mudança de estilo de vida, incluindo dieta equilibrada e exercícios regulares. Como expressaria isso de forma natural?
correctAnswer: I've completely overhauled my lifestyle — I'm eating clean and working out regularly now.
options: I changed my life completely and now I eat healthy food and do exercises every day.
My lifestyle is different now because I eat better and exercise more than before.
I've completely overhauled my lifestyle — I'm eating clean and working out regularly now.
Now I have a new life with good food and regular exercises that I do always.

questionText: Você precisa informar ao médico que é alérgico a penicilina e que já teve uma reação alérgica grave no passado. Como comunicaria isso?
correctAnswer: I should mention that I have a penicillin allergy — I had a severe anaphylactic reaction to it in the past.
options: I should mention that I have a penicillin allergy — I had a severe anaphylactic reaction to it in the past.
I am allergic to penicillin and one time I had a very bad allergic reaction from it.
Don't give me penicillin because I am allergic and had a dangerous reaction before.
Penicillin is something I am allergic to and I became very ill with it in the past.

questionText: Você quer encorajar um familiar idoso a se manter ativo fisicamente para preservar a saúde. Qual expressão seria mais natural e respeitosa?
correctAnswer: Staying active is so important as we get older — even a short daily walk can make a real difference.
options: You need to exercise because old people must be active for their health to be good.
Staying active is so important as we get older — even a short daily walk can make a real difference.
Being active is good for you because you are old and need to take care of your health.
Exercise every day because it is very important for older people to stay in good health.

questionText: Você quer perguntar ao médico se determinado exame de sangue precisa ser feito em jejum. Qual pergunta seria mais natural?
correctAnswer: Does this blood test require me to fast beforehand, and if so, for how long?
options: For this blood test, do I need to not eat before? How many hours I need to fast?
Does this blood test require me to fast beforehand, and if so, for how long?
I need to know if I can eat before doing this blood test or if fasting is needed.
Tell me if I must do fasting before this blood test and for how much time.

questionText: Você está explicando ao médico que o estresse do trabalho tem causado dores de cabeça frequentes e tensão muscular. Como diria isso?
correctAnswer: The stress from work has been triggering frequent headaches and a lot of muscle tension.
options: Work stress is giving me headaches many times and my muscles are very tense because of it.
The stress from work has been triggering frequent headaches and a lot of muscle tension.
Because of work stress I have headaches often and my muscles feel tension also.
I have headaches and muscle tension that come from the stress I have at my work.

questionText: Você quer pedir ao médico que explique os riscos e benefícios de uma cirurgia antes de tomar uma decisão. Como faria esse pedido?
correctAnswer: Before I make any decision, I'd like you to walk me through the risks and benefits of the procedure.
options: Tell me about the good and bad things of this surgery so I can decide what to do.
Before I make any decision, I'd like you to walk me through the risks and benefits of the procedure.
I want to know all the risks and benefits of the surgery before I decide about it.
Can you explain to me the good and bad things about this operation I need to make?

questionText: Você está informando ao médico que tem tomado suplementos vitamínicos por conta própria e quer saber se há alguma contraindicação. Como expressaria isso?
correctAnswer: I've been self-medicating with vitamin supplements — are there any contraindications I should be aware of?
options: I take vitamins by myself and I want to know if there are problems with this for me.
I've been self-medicating with vitamin supplements — are there any contraindications I should be aware of?
I am using vitamin supplements alone and I need to know if this can be bad for me.
Can you tell me if the vitamins I take by myself have any problems with my health?

questionText: Você está numa consulta e o médico menciona um procedimento invasivo. Você quer saber se há alternativas menos invasivas disponíveis. Como perguntaria de forma apropriada para o nível C2?
correctAnswer: Are there any less invasive alternatives we could explore before committing to that procedure?
options: Is there something less invasive we could try first before doing that procedure?
Are there any less invasive alternatives we could explore before committing to that procedure?
Could we consider a non-invasive option prior to undertaking that particular procedure?
Do we have to do this invasive thing or can I try other treatments before it?

questionText: Você precisa comunicar ao seu empregador, de forma profissional, que precisa se ausentar do trabalho para tratar de um problema de saúde crônico que requer acompanhamento médico regular. Como faria isso?
correctAnswer: I need to let you know that I have a chronic condition requiring ongoing medical management, which may necessitate periodic absences.
options: I need to let you know that I have a chronic condition requiring ongoing medical management, which may necessitate periodic absences.
I have a health problem that is chronic and I will need to miss work sometimes for doctor visits.
My chronic health issue means I must attend medical appointments regularly and may be absent from work.
I want to inform you that I am chronically ill and will need time off work to see my doctors.

questionText: Você está discutindo com um médico a possibilidade de um diagnóstico de saúde mental e quer expressar que os sintomas descritos se encaixam na sua experiência. Como diria isso de forma sofisticada?
correctAnswer: What you're describing resonates strongly with what I've been experiencing — it does seem to align with the symptoms you've outlined.
options: Yes, what you say is correct and matches what I have been feeling for a long time now.
What you're describing resonates strongly with what I've been experiencing — it does seem to align with the symptoms you've outlined.
That is exactly what happens to me, the symptoms you describe are what I feel also.
I think your diagnosis is right because I have all the symptoms that you are describing.

questionText: Você está numa consulta e quer expressar ao médico que está relutante em tomar medicação de longo prazo e preferiria explorar mudanças no estilo de vida primeiro. Como expressaria essa preferência?
correctAnswer: I'm somewhat hesitant about committing to long-term medication — I'd rather explore lifestyle modifications as a first-line approach.
options: I don't want to take medicine for a long time. I prefer to change my lifestyle first.
Long-term medication is not something I want. Can we try lifestyle changes before that?
I'm somewhat hesitant about committing to long-term medication — I'd rather explore lifestyle modifications as a first-line approach.
Before taking medication for long, I would like to try changing my habits and lifestyle.

questionText: Você está aconselhando um amigo sobre a importância de manter a saúde mental, especialmente durante períodos de grande estresse. Qual expressão seria mais adequada ao nível C2?
correctAnswer: It's crucial to be proactive about your mental wellbeing, especially when you're under sustained pressure — don't let it fester.
options: You need to take care of your mental health, especially when you are stressed, or it will get worse.
Mental health is very important and you should not ignore it when you have stress in your life.
It's crucial to be proactive about your mental wellbeing, especially when you're under sustained pressure — don't let it fester.
When you are very stressed you must think about your mental health and take care of it more.

questionText: Você está discutindo com seu médico os resultados de exames que indicam níveis elevados de colesterol. Quer expressar preocupação genuína e disposição para mudar. Como faria isso de forma sofisticada?
correctAnswer: I'm genuinely concerned about these cholesterol levels and I'm fully committed to making whatever dietary and lifestyle changes are necessary.
options: I'm genuinely concerned about these cholesterol levels and I'm fully committed to making whatever dietary and lifestyle changes are necessary.
My cholesterol is high and I am worried. I will change my diet and exercise to fix this problem.
These cholesterol results worry me a lot and I want to change my food and my habits to improve.
I am concerned with my high cholesterol and I will do everything to change my lifestyle and diet.

questionText: Você está descrevendo ao psicólogo que sente uma sensação avassaladora de ansiedade em situações sociais que o impede de funcionar normalmente. Como expressaria isso?
correctAnswer: I experience an overwhelming sense of anxiety in social situations that genuinely impairs my ability to function.
options: I feel very anxious when I am with people and this stops me from doing normal things.
Social situations make me very anxious and I cannot function well because of this feeling.
I experience an overwhelming sense of anxiety in social situations that genuinely impairs my ability to function.
When I am in social situations I have so much anxiety that I can't act normally at all.

questionText: Você está tentando convencer um amigo cético sobre os benefícios comprovados da acupuntura para tratamento de dores crônicas. Como argumentaria de forma convincente?
correctAnswer: There's a growing body of evidence supporting acupuncture's efficacy in managing chronic pain — it's worth keeping an open mind.
options: Acupuncture really works for chronic pain and there is much scientific proof of this benefit.
Many studies show that acupuncture helps with chronic pain so you should try it and believe.
There's a growing body of evidence supporting acupuncture's efficacy in managing chronic pain — it's worth keeping an open mind.
Acupuncture has been proven to help chronic pain in many studies so it is not fake medicine.

questionText: Você está informando ao médico que os sintomas que sente variam em intensidade e não seguem um padrão previsível. Como descreveria essa irregularidade de forma precisa?
correctAnswer: The symptoms fluctuate considerably in intensity and don't seem to follow any discernible pattern.
options: My symptoms change a lot in how strong they are and there is no pattern I can see.
The symptoms fluctuate considerably in intensity and don't seem to follow any discernible pattern.
Sometimes my symptoms are strong and sometimes they are weak, without a clear pattern.
I have symptoms that are sometimes worse and sometimes better with no regular pattern at all.

questionText: Você está discutindo com o médico sobre a necessidade de uma segunda opinião antes de decidir sobre um tratamento cirúrgico. Como expressaria esse desejo de forma educada mas firme?
correctAnswer: I hope you understand, but I feel it would be prudent to seek a second opinion before proceeding with surgery.
options: I want another doctor's opinion before I decide about this surgery, if you don't mind.
I hope you understand, but I feel it would be prudent to seek a second opinion before proceeding with surgery.
Before doing the surgery I think it is important to consult another doctor for their opinion.
Can I see another doctor first to get their opinion before we decide about the surgery?

questionText: Você está discutindo com um amigo médico a diferença entre sintomas de esgotamento profissional e depressão clínica. Como iniciaria essa distinção de forma sofisticada?
correctAnswer: There's often a fine line between burnout and clinical depression — the overlap in symptomatology can make differential diagnosis quite challenging.
options: Burnout and depression are different things but they have similar symptoms that can confuse people.
It is hard to know the difference between burnout and depression because the symptoms are similar.
There's often a fine line between burnout and clinical depression — the overlap in symptomatology can make differential diagnosis quite challenging.
Burnout looks like depression sometimes because they share many symptoms and it is hard to tell them apart.

questionText: Você está aconselhando um familiar sobre a importância de aderir rigorosamente ao tratamento prescrito para hipertensão, mesmo quando se sentir bem. Como argumentaria isso?
correctAnswer: It's essential that you stick to the prescribed regimen even when you feel fine — hypertension is largely asymptomatic and the medication works preventively.
options: You must take your hypertension medicine even when you feel okay because it prevents problems.
It's essential that you stick to the prescribed regimen even when you feel fine — hypertension is largely asymptomatic and the medication works preventively.
Even if you feel good you should take your blood pressure medicine because it works to prevent disease.
Hypertension medicine must be taken always even when symptoms are not there because it works for prevention.

questionText: Você está descrevendo ao médico que sua qualidade de vida diminuiu significativamente devido a uma condição crônica e que isso está afetando seu bem-estar emocional. Como expressaria isso?
correctAnswer: My chronic condition has significantly eroded my quality of life, and the emotional toll it's taking is becoming increasingly difficult to cope with.
options: My chronic problem is making my life worse and I feel emotionally bad because of it now.
Because of my chronic condition my life quality is lower and I feel emotionally affected by this.
My chronic condition has significantly eroded my quality of life, and the emotional toll it's taking is becoming increasingly difficult to cope with.
The chronic condition reduced my quality of life a lot and it is emotionally difficult to deal with.

questionText: Você está num debate sobre políticas de saúde pública e quer argumentar que medidas preventivas são mais custo-efetivas do que tratamentos reativos. Como articularia esse argumento?
correctAnswer: Preventive healthcare measures are demonstrably more cost-effective in the long run than reactive treatment — the investment in prevention yields far greater returns.
options: Preventing diseases is cheaper than treating them, so governments should invest more in prevention.
Preventive healthcare measures are demonstrably more cost-effective in the long run than reactive treatment — the investment in prevention yields far greater returns.
It costs less to prevent diseases than to treat them, and this is why prevention policies are better.
Prevention is more economical than treatment in healthcare, so we should focus on preventive measures.

questionText: Você está tentando articular ao seu médico uma sensação física difícil de descrever — uma espécie de pressão intermitente no peito que não chega a ser dor mas causa desconforto. Como expressaria isso com precisão?
correctAnswer: It's not quite pain — more of an intermittent pressure or tightness in my chest that I find rather unsettling.
options: I feel something in my chest that is not pain but like pressure sometimes and it bothers me.
It's not quite pain — more of an intermittent pressure or tightness in my chest that I find rather unsettling.
There is a strange feeling in my chest, not pain exactly, but pressure that comes and goes.
My chest has a feeling of pressure sometimes, not really pain but uncomfortable and worrying.

questionText: Você está num grupo de apoio e quer expressar que a jornada de recuperação de uma doença grave mudou fundamentalmente sua perspectiva sobre a vida. Como articularia isso?
correctAnswer: Going through this illness has been profoundly transformative — it's fundamentally shifted my priorities and given me an entirely new perspective on life.
options: Being sick changed my life and now I see things differently and have different priorities.
Going through this illness has been profoundly transformative — it's fundamentally shifted my priorities and given me an entirely new perspective on life.
My illness changed my perspective completely and now I have new priorities in life.
After being sick I changed a lot and my perspective on life and priorities are all different now.

questionText: Você está questionando um médico sobre a evidência científica por trás de um tratamento experimental que ele recomendou. Como faria essa indagação de forma sofisticada e assertiva?
correctAnswer: I'd be interested to know what the current evidence base looks like for this treatment — are there robust clinical trials supporting its efficacy?
options: Is there scientific proof that this experimental treatment works? I want to see the evidence.
What studies show that this treatment is effective? I want to know the scientific basis for it.
I'd be interested to know what the current evidence base looks like for this treatment — are there robust clinical trials supporting its efficacy?
Can you show me the research that proves this experimental treatment is good and safe for me?

questionText: Você está discutindo com um especialista em saúde pública as implicações éticas da vacinação compulsória versus a autonomia individual. Como introduziria esse argumento com nuance?
correctAnswer: The tension between individual autonomy and collective immunity raises profound ethical questions that resist simplistic resolution.
options: The tension between individual autonomy and collective immunity raises profound ethical questions that resist simplistic resolution.
It is ethically difficult to force people to vaccinate because individual freedom is very important in society.
Mandatory vaccination versus personal choice is a complex ethical debate without easy answers for everyone.
The conflict between forcing vaccination and respecting personal freedom creates difficult ethical dilemmas for public health.

questionText: Você está discutindo com um médico a possibilidade de que seus sintomas sejam psicossomáticos e quer expressar que leva essa possibilidade a sério sem se sentir diminuído por isso. Como articularia isso?
correctAnswer: I'm open to the possibility that there's a psychosomatic component — the mind-body connection is well-established and I wouldn't dismiss that dimension.
options: I know my symptoms could be psychological and I accept that this is possible and important.
I'm open to the possibility that there's a psychosomatic component — the mind-body connection is well-established and I wouldn't dismiss that dimension.
Maybe my symptoms are psychosomatic and I accept this possibility without feeling bad about it.
I understand that psychological factors can cause physical symptoms and I take this seriously as a possibility.

questionText: Você está apresentando um argumento em uma conferência médica sobre a necessidade de uma abordagem mais holística ao tratamento de doenças crônicas. Como iniciaria sua argumentação de forma mais sofisticada?
correctAnswer: The reductionist biomedical model, while invaluable, falls short when addressing the multidimensional nature of chronic illness — we need a more integrative, biopsychosocial framework.
options: We need to treat chronic diseases in a more complete way that considers the whole person, not just the body.
The reductionist biomedical model, while invaluable, falls short when addressing the multidimensional nature of chronic illness — we need a more integrative, biopsychosocial framework.
Chronic diseases require a holistic approach that goes beyond just treating physical symptoms to include mental health.
A holistic approach to chronic disease treatment is better than focusing only on the physical aspects of illness.

questionText: Você está discutindo com um colega médico sobre as limitações do modelo atual de atendimento primário de saúde. Como expressaria uma crítica fundamentada e nuançada?
correctAnswer: Primary care is increasingly overburdened and under-resourced, which inevitably compromises the quality of patient-centred care and drives avoidable secondary referrals.
options: Primary healthcare has too many patients and not enough resources, so the quality of care suffers.
Primary care is increasingly overburdened and under-resourced, which inevitably compromises the quality of patient-centred care and drives avoidable secondary referrals.
The primary care system is overwhelmed with patients and lacks resources, leading to poor quality care.
Our primary care model fails patients because doctors are too busy and there are not enough resources available.

questionText: Você está aconselhando um paciente em remissão de câncer sobre a importância do acompanhamento pós-tratamento e vigilância de recidivas. Como expressaria isso com sensibilidade e precisão técnica?
correctAnswer: Post-remission surveillance is critical — we need to remain vigilant for any signs of recurrence, while also supporting your ongoing recovery and quality of life.
options: After remission we must watch carefully for cancer coming back while also helping you live well.
Post-remission surveillance is critical — we need to remain vigilant for any signs of recurrence, while also supporting your ongoing recovery and quality of life.
Now that you are in remission, we need to monitor you carefully for any cancer recurrence.
It is very important to continue monitoring after remission to check if the cancer comes back.

questionText: Você está mediando uma discussão sobre os determinantes sociais da saúde e quer introduzir a ideia de que a pobreza é em si um fator patogênico. Como articularia esse conceito?
correctAnswer: Poverty functions as a pathogenic force in its own right — its cascading effects on nutrition, housing, stress and healthcare access are well-documented determinants of poor health outcomes.
options: Being poor makes people sick because they don't have good food, housing or access to healthcare.
Poverty is bad for health because poor people lack access to good nutrition and healthcare services.
Poverty functions as a pathogenic force in its own right — its cascading effects on nutrition, housing, stress and healthcare access are well-documented determinants of poor health outcomes.
Social determinants like poverty cause health problems because they affect nutrition, housing and healthcare access.

questionText: Você está conversando com um oncologista sobre o prognóstico de um diagnóstico de câncer e quer entender as implicações estatísticas das taxas de sobrevivência sem soar ansioso ou desinformado. Como formularia essa pergunta?
correctAnswer: Could you contextualise the survival statistics for me — I understand these are population-level figures and don't predetermine individual outcomes, but I'd like a realistic picture.
options: What are my chances of survival? I know statistics are not exact but I want to know the numbers.
Could you contextualise the survival statistics for me — I understand these are population-level figures and don't predetermine individual outcomes, but I'd like a realistic picture.
Can you explain the survival rates to me in a way that shows what they mean for my case?
Please tell me the survival statistics for my cancer type and explain what they mean for me personally.

questionText: Você está discutindo com um colega a questão da medicalização excessiva de condições que antes eram consideradas parte normal da experiência humana. Como iniciaria esse argumento de forma academicamente precisa?
correctAnswer: There's a compelling case to be made that we've pathologised aspects of ordinary human experience — grief, shyness, and age-related decline being prime examples of this creeping medicalisation.
options: We are turning normal human experiences into diseases, like sadness, shyness and aging, which is wrong.
Modern medicine treats too many normal human experiences as diseases, and this is a serious problem.
There's a compelling case to be made that we've pathologised aspects of ordinary human experience — grief, shyness, and age-related decline being prime examples of this creeping medicalisation.
The medicalisation of normal experiences like grief and shyness is problematic and represents overreach by medicine.

questionText: Você está discutindo com um profissional de saúde mental sobre a estigmatização de transtornos psiquiátricos e seu impacto na busca por tratamento. Como articularia essa preocupação de forma sofisticada?
correctAnswer: The pervasive stigma surrounding psychiatric disorders remains a significant barrier to help-seeking behaviour, perpetuating cycles of untreated illness and social marginalisation.
options: People with mental health problems are discriminated against and this stops them from seeking help.
Stigma against mental illness prevents many people from getting the treatment they need and deserve.
The pervasive stigma surrounding psychiatric disorders remains a significant barrier to help-seeking behaviour, perpetuating cycles of untreated illness and social marginalisation.
Mental health stigma is a major problem that stops people from getting treatment for their conditions.

questionText: Você é um médico explicando a um paciente que seu quadro clínico é ambíguo e que os exames não chegaram a resultados conclusivos. Como comunicaria isso com honestidade e sem aumentar a ansiedade do paciente?
correctAnswer: The test results are somewhat inconclusive at this stage — I want to be transparent with you rather than offer a premature diagnosis that may not reflect the full picture.
options: Your tests don't show clear results yet, and I want to be honest with you about this uncertainty.
The test results are somewhat inconclusive at this stage — I want to be transparent with you rather than offer a premature diagnosis that may not reflect the full picture.
I cannot give you a definitive diagnosis yet because the tests are not conclusive enough to be certain.
Your test results are not clear enough for a diagnosis and I think we need to do more tests.

questionText: Você está argumentando em favor da integração de cuidados paliativos precoces no tratamento de doenças terminais. Como apresentaria esse argumento de forma clinicamente fundamentada?
correctAnswer: Early integration of palliative care has been shown to improve quality of life, reduce aggressive end-of-life interventions, and paradoxically, in some studies, even extend survival.
options: Palliative care should start early in terminal illness because it improves quality of life and reduces suffering.
Starting palliative care early helps terminal patients live better and with less aggressive treatment at the end.
Early palliative care integration improves quality of life for terminal patients and reduces unnecessary interventions.
Early integration of palliative care has been shown to improve quality of life, reduce aggressive end-of-life interventions, and paradoxically, in some studies, even extend survival.

questionText: Você está participando de um debate sobre ética biomédica e quer defender o princípio da autonomia do paciente contra uma visão excessivamente paternalista da medicina. Como articularia esse argumento?
correctAnswer: Patient autonomy must be sacrosanct — a paternalistic approach, however well-intentioned, ultimately infantilises patients and undermines the therapeutic relationship.
options: Patients have the right to make their own decisions about their health and doctors should respect this.
Patient autonomy must be sacrosanct — a paternalistic approach, however well-intentioned, ultimately infantilises patients and undermines the therapeutic relationship.
Medical paternalism is wrong because patients have the right to decide about their own treatment.
Doctors should not be paternalistic because patients have the right to make their own health decisions.

questionText: Você está descrevendo numa consulta psiquiátrica que sua sensação de desrealização ocorre de forma episódica, frequentemente precipitada por privação de sono ou hiperventilação. Como articularia isso com precisão clínica?
correctAnswer: I experience episodic derealisation, typically precipitated by sleep deprivation or hyperventilation — it's a profoundly disorienting sensation.
options: Sometimes I feel like reality is not real, and this happens when I don't sleep or breathe too fast.
I experience episodic derealisation, typically precipitated by sleep deprivation or hyperventilation — it's a profoundly disorienting sensation.
My episodes of feeling unreal happen when I am very tired or when I breathe quickly, and they are disturbing.
I have feelings of unreality that come in episodes, often when I haven't slept or am breathing fast.

questionText: Você está discutindo as implicações da genômica personalizada na medicina preventiva com um especialista. Como introduziria sua posição sobre os limites éticos do uso de dados genéticos?
correctAnswer: While personalised genomics holds extraordinary promise for preventive medicine, the ethical ramifications of genetic data privacy and the potential for discrimination warrant careful regulatory scrutiny.
options: Genetic data used in medicine is promising but raises serious privacy and discrimination concerns that need regulation.

While personalised genomics holds extraordinary promise for preventive medicine, the ethical ramifications of genetic data privacy and the potential for discrimination warrant careful regulatory scrutiny.
Using personal genetic data in medicine is beneficial but we need to protect privacy and prevent discrimination.
Genomic medicine is very promising but the ethics of genetic data use require careful consideration and regulation.

questionText: Você está descrevendo ao médico que seus sintomas têm uma natureza cíclica e parecem correlacionar-se com mudanças hormonais. Como comunicaria essa observação de forma clinicamente precisa?
correctAnswer: There appears to be a cyclical pattern to my symptoms that seems to correlate with hormonal fluctuations — particularly in the premenstrual phase.
options: My symptoms come and go in a cycle and I think they are related to my hormones and menstrual cycle.
I notice my symptoms follow a pattern and get worse before my period, so it might be hormonal.
There appears to be a cyclical pattern to my symptoms that seems to correlate with hormonal fluctuations — particularly in the premenstrual phase.
My symptoms are cyclical and happen more before my period, suggesting a hormonal connection to them.

questionText: Você está participando de uma conferência sobre saúde e quer argumentar que a abordagem atual da medicina falha ao não considerar o impacto do trauma na saúde física ao longo da vida. Como argumentaria isso?
correctAnswer: The adverse childhood experiences research has irrefutably demonstrated that unresolved trauma exerts a profound and lasting impact on physical health trajectories — yet this remains woefully underaddressed in clinical practice.
options: Childhood trauma has lasting effects on physical health and medicine does not address this problem enough.
Research shows that trauma in childhood affects physical health throughout life and medicine ignores this.

The adverse childhood experiences research has irrefutably demonstrated that unresolved trauma exerts a profound and lasting impact on physical health trajectories — yet this remains woefully underaddressed in clinical practice.
Unresolved trauma from childhood has proven physical health consequences that clinical medicine still neglects.

questionText: Você está comunicando a um paciente com doença terminal as opções de cuidados de fim de vida, incluindo cuidados paliativos domiciliares. Como introduziria essa conversa difícil com sensibilidade e clareza?
correctAnswer: I know this is an incredibly difficult conversation to have, but I want to make sure we discuss your wishes and explore all the options available to ensure your comfort and dignity.
options: We need to talk about end-of-life care options because your illness is terminal and we must plan now.
I know this is an incredibly difficult conversation to have, but I want to make sure we discuss your wishes and explore all the options available to ensure your comfort and dignity.
Since your illness is terminal, we should now discuss palliative care and what you want at the end.
It is time to talk about end-of-life options so we can plan how to keep you comfortable until the end.
